tweets.text:
The 'less is more' product rule:

TBH had exactly one feature: Anonymous polls.
Gas had exactly one feature: Anonymous compliments.

They didn't add features. They perfected one.

Focus beats breadth.

Vietnam: Top 3 globally for crypto adoption.

Why?
• Remittance corridor to US
• Limited banking access
• High mobile penetration
• USD scarcity

Stablecoins solve real problems.

CBDC offline payments:

• Needed: Rural areas, disasters
• Challenge: Double-spending without connectivity
• Solutions: Hardware wallets, local consensus
• Status: Experimental, not deployed

Hard problem. Important for inclusion.

Uniswap v3 innovation:

• Concentrated liquidity (efficiency)
• Multiple fee tiers (flexibility)
• NFT positions (capital efficiency)

Result: Deeper liquidity, lower slippage.

Innovation compounds.

Web3 gaming state:

• Promise: Own your items, trade freely
• Reality: Most games aren't fun
• Reality: Economies collapse
• Reality: Speculation > gameplay

Fun first. Ownership second. Order matters.

Hash rate = security budget.

Bitcoin: 500+ EH/s (all-time high)
Ethereum (post-merge): Staked ETH = security

Higher hash rate = More expensive to attack.
Security isn't free.

Why teens are the best growth channel:

• Dense networks (schools)
• High sharing behavior
• Identity formation (exploring who they are)
• Time rich, money poor (free apps win)

Build for teens, scale to adults.

Stablecoin regulation incoming:

• EU: MiCA framework (2024)
• US: Draft bills circulating
• Focus: Reserves, audits, licensing

The wild west phase is ending. Compliance becomes competitive advantage.

Product lesson: Constraints breed creativity.

TBH was built by 4 people in 3 months.

Limited resources forced hard choices.
Hard choices created focus.
Focus created success.

Abundance is the enemy of clarity.

Sweden's e-krona:

• Testing since 2020
• Approach: Cautious, research-heavy
• Finding: Cash usage declining
• Decision: Not launching yet

Sometimes not launching is the right decision.
